climate.description: Autumn in Andean foothills: mild days, cool nights. Dry with potential winds.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — no confirmed edition has run since November 3, 2019. When it operated, the long course ran two laps through Ullum Valley's plains and hills, climbing toward the Punta Negra dam via the Interlagos road, finishing in San Juan city center — transitioning from flatlands to Andean foothills on paved roads. The one-lap medium course was timed but explicitly non-competitive.
course_description.signature_challenge: Historically, the Punta Negra dam climb after the Ullum Valley laps — no longer verifiable as current given the event's dormant status.
suffering_zones: Ullum Valley — repeated laps building fatigue before the dam climb (2019 and earlier editions)
Punta Negra Dam — the signature ascent, exposed to wind with Andean views

logistics.lodging_strategy: Numerous hotels in San Juan city center near start line historically served the event; not verified as currently applicable given no confirmed edition since 2019.

history.origin_story: Founding year not verified — the prior 2022 date is impossible, since official GFNY reporting documents a San Juan edition already in 2019, described as the third GFNY Argentina edition. Earlier GFNY Argentina editions may have used a different host city; do not assume a 2017 San Juan founding from the 'third edition' language alone.
notable_moments: 2019: Most recent confirmed edition, November 3, described by GFNY as the third GFNY Argentina edition

biased_opinion.summary: GFNY San Juan is a real event with a genuine history — an Ullum Valley/Punta Negra Dam course that ran at least through November 2019 as GFNY's third Argentina edition. But there is no evidence of a 2020-2026 running, the Argentina microsite currently displays a stale 'TBA Argentina' header with broken schedule content, and the 2026 GFNY global calendar contains no Argentina or San Juan round at all. The March 1, 2026 date this profile previously carried actually belongs to a different race — GFNY Salinas in Ecuador — and should not be read as evidence San Juan is running.
strengths: Historically distinctive Ullum Valley/Punta Negra Dam/Interlagos setting, unlike GFNY's more typical mountain-pass courses
Straightforward city-center San Juan logistics when the event was running
A genuine competitive long course paired with a more approachable, non-competitive medium option
weaknesses: No confirmed edition since November 2019 — this is not a currently bookable event
The organizer's own Argentina microsite is stale and internally inconsistent ('TBA' header, mismatched schedule content)
Moderate rather than marquee mountain climbing compared to GFNY's Alpine/Andean flagship events
The two-lap long course repeats the same Ullum Valley loop, which some riders may find repetitive
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Treat as dormant, not bookable. If a future edition is announced, revisit; until then, this is not a current GFNY option.

final_verdict.one_liner: GFNY's Ullum Valley/Punta Negra Dam course, dormant since its 2019 edition with no confirmed return.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — there is no current edition to register for. GFNY's 2026 global calendar contains no Argentina/San Juan round.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a currently active GFNY South American event, check GFNY's global calendar for a confirmed edition elsewhere in the region.
